topics.title: Mercury
topics.description: Mercury is the closest planet to the Sun. It is a small rocky planet with very hot days and very cold nights.
topics.fact: A year on Mercury is only 88 Earth days.
topics.question: Which planet is closest to the Sun?

topics.title: Venus
topics.description: Venus is the hottest planet in our solar system because its thick clouds trap heat.
topics.fact: Venus spins in the opposite direction from most planets.
topics.question: Which planet is the hottest?

topics.title: Earth
topics.description: Earth is our home planet. It has air, water, land, and the right conditions for life.
topics.fact: About 71 percent of Earth is covered by water.
topics.question: Which planet do humans live on?

topics.title: Mars
topics.description: Mars is called the Red Planet because iron minerals in the soil make it look red.
topics.fact: Mars has a huge volcano named Olympus Mons.
topics.question: Which planet is called the Red Planet?

topics.title: Jupiter
topics.description: Jupiter is the largest planet in the solar system. It is a gas giant and has a giant storm called the Great Red Spot.
topics.question: Which planet is the largest?

topics.title: Saturn
topics.description: Saturn is famous for its beautiful rings made of ice, rock, and dust.
topics.fact: Saturn is a gas giant with very wide rings.
topics.question: Which planet is famous for rings?
